description: In-App Updates — Add user-facing update notifications via electron-updater with status bar indicator, update dialog with release notes and download progress, and settings toggle for auto-check.

userStories.title: Main process update event handling
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the main process to check for updates via electron-updater and expose update state to the renderer via IPC.
acceptanceCriteria: Main process uses autoUpdater from electron-updater to check for updates
autoUpdater.autoDownload is set to false (user must opt in to download)
autoUpdater.autoInstallOnAppQuit is set to true
IPC handler updates:checkForUpdates triggers a manual check and returns { available: boolean, version?: string, releaseNotes?: string }
IPC handler updates:downloadUpdate starts the download and returns immediately
IPC handler updates:installUpdate calls autoUpdater.quitAndInstall()

Main process forwards autoUpdater events to the renderer via webContents.send: update:available (with version + releaseNotes), update:download-progress (with percent, bytesPerSecond, transferred, total), update:downloaded (ready to install), update:error (with error message)
Auto-check runs 10 seconds after app launch (non-blocking), only if the autoCheckUpdates setting is enabled in electron-store (default true)
In dev mode (!app.isPackaged), skip update checks entirely to avoid errors
Typecheck passes, existing tests pass
userStories.notes: Import autoUpdater from 'electron-updater'. It's already in package.json dependencies. Use store.get('autoCheckUpdates', true) to read the setting. autoUpdater events: checking-for-update, update-available, update-not-available, download-progress, update-downloaded, error. The update-available event provides { version, releaseNotes } in its info object. Use BrowserWindow.getAllWindows()[0].webContents.send() to forward events to renderer.

userStories.title: Preload and renderer type definitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need IPC bridge functions and event listeners in the preload script so the renderer can interact with the update system.
acceptanceCriteria: Preload exposes window.electronAPI.updates.checkForUpdates() wrapping ipcRenderer.invoke('updates:checkForUpdates')
Preload exposes window.electronAPI.updates.downloadUpdate() wrapping ipcRenderer.invoke('updates:downloadUpdate')
Preload exposes window.electronAPI.updates.installUpdate() wrapping ipcRenderer.invoke('updates:installUpdate')
Preload exposes window.electronAPI.updates.onUpdateAvailable(callback) using ipcRenderer.on('update:available', ...) and returning a cleanup function
Preload exposes window.electronAPI.updates.onDownloadProgress(callback) using ipcRenderer.on('update:download-progress', ...) and returning a cleanup function
Preload exposes window.electronAPI.updates.onUpdateDownloaded(callback) using ipcRenderer.on('update:downloaded', ...) and returning a cleanup function
Preload exposes window.electronAPI.updates.onUpdateError(callback) using ipcRenderer.on('update:error', ...) and returning a cleanup function
Type definitions added to useLayoutState.ts window.electronAPI block for the updates namespace
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the same pattern as the existing onRepoChanged listener in preload: ipcRenderer.on('channel', handler) with a cleanup function that calls ipcRenderer.removeListener. The callback signatures: onUpdateAvailable receives { version: string, releaseNotes: string }, onDownloadProgress receives { percent: number, bytesPerSecond: number, transferred: number, total: number }, onUpdateDownloaded receives no args, onUpdateError receives { message: string }.

userStories.title: Status bar update indicator
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a subtle indicator in the status bar when an update is available, so I know there's a new version without being interrupted.
acceptanceCriteria: StatusBar component listens for update:available events via window.electronAPI.updates.onUpdateAvailable
When an update is available, a clickable badge appears in the status bar showing the version (e.g. 'v1.2.0 available') with a download icon from Lucide
The badge uses accent color, small font size (var(--font-sm)), fits the existing status bar aesthetic
Clicking the badge dispatches window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('updates:show-dialog', { detail: { version, releaseNotes } }))
The badge is hidden by default and only appears when an update is available
After update is downloaded, badge text changes to 'Restart to update'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: StatusBar.tsx already has useEffect hooks for listening to events. Add the update listener alongside existing ones. Use the Download or ArrowDownToLine icon from lucide-react. Style the badge similar to the ahead/behind badges already in the status bar.

userStories.title: Update dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see what changed in the new version and download it with one click.
acceptanceCriteria: AppLayout renders an UpdateDialog component when updates:show-dialog custom event fires
Dialog shows: current version, new version number, release notes as pre-formatted text, and action buttons
Initial state shows 'Download & Install' and 'Remind Me Later' buttons
Clicking 'Download & Install' calls window.electronAPI.updates.downloadUpdate(), shows a progress bar with percentage
Progress bar updates via onDownloadProgress listener showing percent and download speed (e.g. '45% — 2.3 MB/s')
When download completes (onUpdateDownloaded fires), button changes to 'Restart to Update'
Clicking 'Restart to Update' calls window.electronAPI.updates.installUpdate()
Clicking 'Remind Me Later' or the X close button closes the dialog (status bar badge remains)
If an error occurs during download, show error message with a 'Retry' button
Dialog is a modal overlay with backdrop click to dismiss, styled consistently with existing dialogs in the app
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create a new component src/renderer/src/components/UpdateDialog.tsx. Use the existing dialog overlay pattern from the app (e.g. KeyboardShortcutsPanel or the merge/rebase dialogs use .branch-dialog-overlay). The release notes string may contain markdown — render it as a <pre> block for simplicity (no need for a full markdown renderer). For download speed, format bytes: if > 1MB show as 'X.X MB/s', else 'X KB/s'.

userStories.title: Settings toggle for auto-check
userStories.description: As a user, I want to enable or disable automatic update checks in Settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Add autoCheckUpdates: boolean to AppSettings in useSettings.ts (default true)
Add a 'General' section at the top of SettingsPanel with a 'Check for updates automatically' toggle
Add a 'Check Now' button next to the toggle that calls window.electronAPI.updates.checkForUpdates() and shows inline result: 'Checking...' then 'Up to date!' or 'Update available: vX.Y.Z'
The setting is saved to localStorage via useSettings AND sent to the main process via a new IPC call updates:setAutoCheck so the main process can persist it to electron-store
IPC handler updates:setAutoCheck in main process stores the value in electron-store so it can be read on next startup before the renderer loads
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the existing pattern in useSettings.ts for adding new settings. The SettingsPanel already has styled toggle/checkbox components — reuse those. The 'Check Now' button should be small and inline, similar to the refresh buttons elsewhere in the app. For the IPC handler, just do store.set('autoCheckUpdates', value) in main.
